# The EDM channel plan (regenerated by edm_plan.py)

Channels for multi unit interconnect: one modulated source per transmitting unit, one energy window per receiving port (theory Section 10.4). Companion lines of the same source modulate together and are free redundant copies of their channel, usable as parity. Resolution model: FWHM = a sqrt(E), anchored 2 percent (CZT) and 7 percent (NaI) at 662 keV.

| line (keV) | source | t1/2 (y) | FWHM (keV) | nearest line | separation / FWHM | worst window leakage |

| 59.5 | 241Am | 432 | 4.0 | 57Co (122.1) | 15.8 | 0.0% |
| 122.1 | 57Co | 0.74 | 5.7 | 241Am (59.5) | 11.0 | 0.0% |

| 1115.5 | 65Zn | 0.67 | 17.2 | 60Co (1173.2) | 3.4 | 0.0% |
| 1173.2 | 60Co | 5.27 | 17.6 | 65Zn (1115.5) | 3.3 | 0.0% |

**8 of 8 channels clean** (separation at least 2 FWHM and leakage under 5 percent).

**6 of 8 channels clean** (separation at least 2 FWHM and leakage under 5 percent).

## Longevity note

Channel sources age like everything else here, and the plan says so: ⁵⁷Co, ⁵⁴Mn, and ⁶⁵Zn are annual consumables; ²²Na and ⁶⁰Co are five year parts; ¹³³Ba is a decade part; ¹³⁷Cs and ²⁴¹Am are the mission grade channels. A long mission rack should carry its high traffic links on ²⁴¹Am, ¹³⁷Cs, ¹³³Ba, and ⁶⁰Co, and treat the rest as replaceable patch cables. By degree homogeneity (theory Section 7), channel aging costs link bandwidth, never link correctness, provided both ends ratio against the same line.
